notes: Both tables lint 1:1 against FIDELITY.md; FIDELITY_MATRIX.md stays a legacy pinned matrix. The payment-collection chain (F-731: PI lifecycle incl. card decline/retry + settlement/ledger/event reads), the customer-management chain (F-732), and the refunds chain (F-733 reconciliation: ledger + events closed, docs synced) carry their ruled heat (hot, F-729); the warm set lands with F-734. Disputes are unlisted cold per the F-729 ruled list (catch-all 501), not warm — see the F-733 [DECISION].

tools.name: create_payment_intent
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): both rails — x402 crypto deposit and card (F-731), incl. one-shot create+confirm.

tools.name: retrieve_payment_intent
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): the post-attempt read agents poll for status and last_payment_error.

tools.name: list_payment_intents
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729); cursor pagination on (created, id).

tools.name: confirm_payment_intent
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): card confirms attempt the charge synchronously with magic-PM declines (F-731); crypto confirm stays the documented idempotent no-op.

tools.name: update_payment_intent
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment(card) (ruled F-729, ruling point 1): the retry-with-new-PM step after a decline (F-731).

tools.name: cancel_payment_intent
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): abandon step; CAS-guarded, refused once succeeded.

tools.name: simulate_crypto_deposit
tools.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729); SB: the sealed twin's x402 settlement entry point.

tools.name: retrieve_charge
tools.justification: TC:settlement-verify (ruled F-729): agents read the minted (or failed, F-731) charge to verify the attempt.

tools.name: list_charges
tools.justification: TC:settlement-verify (ruled F-729); payment_intent / customer / created filters.

tools.name: retrieve_balance
tools.justification: TC:ledger-check (ruled F-729): available/pending reflect settled PIs.

tools.name: list_balance_transactions
tools.justification: TC:ledger-check (ruled F-729): the ledger entries minted by settles and refunds.

tools.name: retrieve_event
tools.justification: TC:poll-confirm (ruled F-729): no webhook delivery in v1, agents poll events.

tools.name: list_events
tools.justification: TC:poll-confirm (ruled F-729): the polling read; type + created filters.

tools.name: create_refund
tools.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729); MCP:create_refund — the only resource keeping a dedicated tool on Stripe's official MCP server. F-733: mints the negative refund-type ledger entry and emits charge.refunded + refund.created in one transaction.

tools.name: retrieve_refund
tools.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729): the post-refund verify read.

tools.name: list_refunds
tools.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729); charge + payment_intent filters.

tools.name: create_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729): customer management is the precondition of nearly every collection chain. Vendor MCP has meta-tools only; task chain wins per rubric.

tools.name: retrieve_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729).

tools.name: update_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); Stripe per-key metadata merge modeled semantically.

tools.name: delete_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); soft delete serves the {deleted: true} stub and detaches PMs like real Stripe.

tools.name: list_customers
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); cursor pagination + email filter.

tools.name: list_customer_payment_methods
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): the customer-scoped PM list is the hot read; the top-level PM list stays cold.

tools.name: create_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): card PMs from test card numbers; PAN never stored. Magic decline PMs drive the F-731 card retry chain.

tools.name: retrieve_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

tools.name: attach_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): one customer per PM; a detached PM can never be reattached (Stripe lifecycle rule).

tools.name: detach_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): both rails — x402 crypto deposit and card (F-731), incl. one-shot create+confirm.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents/:id
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): the post-attempt read agents poll for status and last_payment_error.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729); cursor pagination on (created, id).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents/:id/confirm
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): card confirms attempt the charge synchronously with magic-PM declines (F-731); crypto confirm stays the documented idempotent no-op.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents/:id
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment(card) (ruled F-729, ruling point 1): update — the retry-with-new-PM step after a decline (F-731).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_intents/:id/cancel
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729): abandon step; CAS-guarded, refused once succeeded.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/test_helpers/payment_intents/:id/simulate_crypto_deposit
rest.justification: TC:collect-payment (ruled F-729); SB: the sealed twin's x402 settlement entry point.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/charges/:id
rest.justification: TC:settlement-verify (ruled F-729): agents read the minted (or failed, F-731) charge to verify the attempt.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/charges
rest.justification: TC:settlement-verify (ruled F-729); payment_intent / customer / created filters.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/balance
rest.justification: TC:ledger-check (ruled F-729): available/pending reflect settled PIs.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/balance_transactions
rest.justification: TC:ledger-check (ruled F-729): the ledger entries minted by settles and refunds.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/events/:id
rest.justification: TC:poll-confirm (ruled F-729): no webhook delivery in v1, agents poll events.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/events
rest.justification: TC:poll-confirm (ruled F-729): the polling read; type + created filters.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/refunds
rest.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729); MCP:create_refund corroborates. F-733: full/partial refunds, over-refund + failed-charge refusals, negative refund-type ledger entry, charge.refunded + refund.created events — all in one transaction. Refund partial ops (POST /v1/refunds/:id, /cancel) stay unlisted per F-729 ruling point 4.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/refunds/:id
rest.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729): the post-refund verify read.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/refunds
rest.justification: TC:refund (ruled F-729); charge + payment_intent filters. Omits the legacy count field (FIDELITY.md divergence #9).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/customers
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729): customer management is the precondition of nearly every collection chain.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); deleted customers serve the {deleted: true} stub like real Stripe.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); cursor pagination + email filter, deleted rows excluded.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); Stripe per-key metadata merge (empty value unsets).

rest.name: DELETE /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); soft delete detaches the customer's PMs in the same transaction.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id/payment_methods
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): the customer-scoped PM list is the hot read; the top-level GET /v1/payment_methods stays cold (loud 501).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): card PMs from test card numbers (Luhn + expiry card_errors); PAN never stored. Magic decline PMs drive the F-731 card retry chain.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id/attach
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): one customer per PM; a detached PM can never be reattached.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id/detach
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).
